other_observers: Mark Chavez, Joe Roller, Loch Kilpatrick
description: An empidonax flycatcher based on size, olive and yellow color, wingbars and tail flicking.  The bird was sulphur yellow below, including throat and malar, with dark gray wings with buffy wingbars.  The bird has a rounded head and near complete, round, yellowish eyering.  The primary projection was moderte to long.  The tail was dark and somewhat short.  The bird was colored much like a Cordilleran Flycatcher but structurally was more similar to a Least Flycatcher, but longer winged.  The pale edges of the secondaries did not extend all the way to the lower wingbar (pale tips of greater coverts).  The wing was dark, but not as black as an adult of this species and the wingbars were buffy, suggesting an immature bird.  See photos.
photographer: Photographer: Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek
